title: Floor plan of Chiswick House with additional wings
creator: Wikimedia user Chivalrick1
research_notes: Modern measured/diagram redraw of an 18th-century villa; clean labeled plan. CC BY-SA requires attribution.
research_features: Neo-Palladian villa with a central octagonal domed saloon (polygonal room) and a strictly symmetric suite of variously shaped rooms (circular, apsidal, octagonal) plus flanking wings. Mixed polygonal/curved interior geometry.
feature_highlights: central domed saloon flanked by a suite of distinct room shapes
apsidal (curved-end) rooms and small circular closets
octagonal and other straight-sided polygonal rooms
two spiral staircases drawn as curved coils
numbered-room key instead of inscribed dimensions
symmetric layout with two flanking pavilion-wings and an exterior double stair
supported_examples: straight-sided polygonal rooms (octagonal and angular) via custom room polygons
symmetric room arrangement with mirrored pairs
straight walls at varied angles forming the wing blocks
raster underlay tracing of a measured diagram
roadmap_examples: wall construction profiles / poché for thick masonry walls
straight-run and (eventually) stair topology for the exterior double stair

gap_features.why: apsidal curved-end rooms, round closets, and spiral-stair enclosures need true arc walls, not straight segments
rooms are identified by small numbers keyed to a separate legend rather than by drawn dimensions
the plan carries a graphic SCALE OF FEET bar as a drawn annotation
